prevalence.source: European urticaria epidemiology syntheses / EAACI context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Urticaria/angioedema >6 weeks; evaluation guided by history; management per EAACI/GA2LEN/EuroGuiDerm guidance

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with stepwise therapy and biologic access; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialist allergy/dermatology pathways common in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antihistamine up-dosing, omalizumab for refractory CSU, cyclosporine in selected cases; short steroid bursts for flares
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong guideline pathways and broad access to specialist services in many systems
